title: Kushmanda Chalisa
intro: Shri Kushmanda Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Kushmanda
description:
|| Shri Kushmanda Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Mata Kushmanda ki jai, karuna ki saagar.
Sukh-sampatti dene wali, jai maa, jai ambar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati jagat ki mata.
Kushmanda devi sukhdayi bhrata.
Shumbh-Nishumbh harani mata.
Bhakton ki vipada harani bhrata.
Kush (kumar) mand harsh se bhari.
Charon or kripa ki jhari.
Hans par savar he mata.
Kripa ka aviral bahata.
Kumbh karon mein jal se bhare.
Dhan-dhanya se bhare saare ghar.
Aabha se dipt he mata.
Bhakton ke sankat mitata.
Chaturthi tithi shubh kehlati.
Vrat rakhne se sab safal hoti.
Mantra ka jap karo he pyare.
Jeevan sukhi ho jaye saare.
Ant mein sun lo araj hamari.
Jeevan saware bhavsagar se.
Jai Mata Kushmanda Bhavani.
Kripa karo he jagat ki rani.

|| Iti Shri Kushmanda Chalisa Sampurna ||
meaning:
|| Shri Kushmanda Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Mata Kushmanda ki jai, karuna ki saagar.
(Victory to Mother Kushmanda, the ocean of compassion.)
Sukh-sampatti dene wali, jai maa, jai ambar.
(Giver of happiness and wealth, victory to Mother, victory to the sky/limitless Mother.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati jagat ki mata.
(Victory, victory to the Mother of the world.)
Kushmanda devi sukhdayi bhrata.
(Goddess Kushmanda, the giver of happiness.)
Shumbh-Nishumbh harani mata.
(Mother, destroyer of Shumbh and Nishumbh.)
Bhakton ki vipada harani bhrata.
(Remover of the devotees' troubles.)
Kush (kumar) mand harsh se bhari.
(Filled with the joy of your gentle smile.)
Charon or kripa ki jhari.
(A shower of grace in all directions.)
Hans par savar he mata.
(O Mother, riding on a swan.)
Kripa ka aviral bahata.
(Flowing incessantly with grace.)
Kumbh karon mein jal se bhare.
(Your hands are filled with pots of water.)
Dhan-dhanya se bhare saare ghar.
(Filling all homes with wealth and grain.)
Aabha se dipt he mata.
(O Mother, glowing with radiance.)
Bhakton ke sankat mitata.
(Erasing the troubles of devotees.)
Chaturthi tithi shubh kehlati.
(The fourth day is known as auspicious.)
Vrat rakhne se sab safal hoti.
(Fasting on this day makes everything successful.)
Mantra ka jap karo he pyare.
(Chant the mantra, O dear ones.)
Jeevan sukhi ho jaye saare.
(May all of life become happy.)
Ant mein sun lo araj hamari.
(In the end, hear our prayer.)
Jeevan saware bhavsagar se.
(Save and adorn our life from the ocean of existence.)
Jai Mata Kushmanda Bhavani.
(Victory to Mother Kushmanda Bhavani.)
Kripa karo he jagat ki rani.
(Bestow your grace, O Queen of the world.)

|| Iti Shri Kushmanda Chalisa Sampurna ||

faq.a1: 'Ku' means little, 'Ushma' means warmth/energy, and 'Anda' means cosmic egg or universe. It signifies that the Goddess created the entire universe with a gentle smile.
faq.q2: What is the main benefit of worshiping her?
faq.a2: Worshiping Maa Kushmanda bestows longevity, fame, and strength. She removes ailments and sorrows from her devotees' lives and grants them good health.
faq.a3: Maa Kushmanda governs the 'Anahata Chakra' (Heart Chakra). Her worship helps in filling the heart with love, compassion, and positivity.
faq.q4: When is the auspicious day to worship her?
faq.a4: The fourth day of Navratri (Chaturthi) is dedicated to Maa Kushmanda.
faq.q5: What is the appropriate offering (Bhog) for her?
faq.a5: 'Malpua' is considered her favorite offering. It is believed that offering Malpua pleases the Goddess, and she blesses her devotees with happiness and prosperity.
